description: Validate one-dimensional rigid-body momentum transfer across a contact event using explicit mass ratio, restitution, and post-collision velocity evidence. The two-body collision is only a smoke family.
physical_assumptions: The moving active body transfers momentum to a passive receiver through runtime contact.
Both bodies must declare positive mass values before runtime.
Passive receivers must start still and move only after contact.
Post-collision velocity ordering should match the declared mass ratio and restitution envelope.
Total kinetic energy should not grow beyond the restitution-bounded proxy envelope.
verifier_rules: Active and passive bodies declare positive mass.
Passive body initial velocity is near zero.
Post-contact target velocity is in the declared direction and bounded range.
Velocity ordering follows heavy-to-light or light-to-heavy mass ratio expectations.
Kinetic energy does not exceed the allowed energy-gain ratio.
repair_suggestions: Declare mass_kg on both colliding bodies.
Remove hidden initial velocity from passive receivers.
Export the active-passive contact event before checking post-collision velocities.
Use restitution and mass ratio to derive expected velocity bounds instead of hard-coded target motion.
